subject: (no subject)
body:
Darren said frankly  he thought you didn't want me involved . he and gideon opened an acct with citi.. the trader told darren that he could buy no more than an additional 50 -100 thousand shares without moving the market.even if he had a larger order , talk to you tomorrow. he said david faber he, and bramman thought the number should be 100k

FYI

Sent from my iPhone

Begin forwarded message:


From: "Caplan, David L." <david.caplan@dpw.com>
Date: November 22, 2007 4:11:40 PM EST
To: <dkiesq@aol.com>, <tfaber@limitedbrands.com>, <sfried@limited.com>, <bsoll@limitedbrands.com>, <abramman@limitedbrands.com>
Cc: <awexner@limitedbrands.com>
Subject: Final Decision re Wexner Trust


Per Abigail, final decision is to implement the original arrangement: buy in the mkt tomorrow without disrupting mkt activity with the remainder (1 million less the shares bought tomorrow) to be purchased thru a 10b5-1 plan. Darren, Tim and Anne, I assume you'll finalize with Citi?  Pls let me know if there's anything I can do. I will be reachable via email and cellphone (917 750 5820).  Thanks very much--and Happy Thanksgiving.
--------------------------
Sent from my BlackBerry Wireless Device
